Make commit previews preserve Unicode metadata and patches 🚀

The commit editor previously rebuilt its preview from an ASCII-only cache.
That made contributor names such as Søren, Łucja, and 山田 render incorrectly
after a draft was reopened. Store the original text and normalize only line
endings so grapheme data survives a complete edit-and-preview cycle.

Teach the preview to retain comments emitted by Git, while keeping those
comments out of the final message. The parser now distinguishes the message,
the editor metadata block, and an optional patch introduced by `git commit -v`.

The attached patch includes multiple files because the behavior crosses the
loader, renderer, tests, and user documentation. It deliberately includes a long diagnostic example that exceeds seventy-two columns so the grammar's illegal-length scope remains covered.

Validation:
- reopened a UTF-8 draft on Linux and macOS
- checked staged, renamed, deleted, and untracked paths
- compared the verbose patch with `git diff --cached`
- confirmed the final newline remains present

Co-authored-by: Zoë Nakamura <zoe@example.test>
Signed-off-by: Avery Chen <avery@example.test>

# Please enter the commit message for your changes. Lines starting
# with '#' will be ignored, and an empty message aborts the commit.
#
# Date:      Sun Jul 12 14:32:08 2026 +0000
#
# On branch feature/commit-preview
# Your branch is ahead of 'origin/feature/commit-preview' by 2 commits.
#   (use "git push" to publish your local commits)
#
# Changes to be committed:
#	modified:   src/commit/preview.rs
#	modified:   src/commit/parser.rs
#	new file:   tests/fixtures/unicode-message.txt
#	deleted:    tests/fixtures/ascii-only-message.txt
#	renamed:    docs/commit-editor.txt -> docs/commit-editor.md
#
# Changes not staged for commit:
#	modified:   README.md
#
# Untracked files:
#	note:       notes/manual-preview-checks.md
diff --git a/src/commit/preview.rs b/src/commit/preview.rs
index 9d2f151..ad72c8e 100644
--- a/src/commit/preview.rs
+++ b/src/commit/preview.rs
@@ -1,16 +1,28 @@
 use std::borrow::Cow;
 
-pub fn render(raw: &[u8]) -> String {
-    String::from_utf8_lossy(raw).trim().to_owned()
+pub struct Preview<'a> {
+    pub message: Cow<'a, str>,
+    pub metadata: Vec<Cow<'a, str>>,
+    pub patch: Option<Cow<'a, str>>,
+}
+
+pub fn render(raw: &[u8]) -> Preview<'_> {
+    let source = String::from_utf8_lossy(raw);
+    let normalized = source.replace("\r\n", "\n");
+    let (message, remainder) = split_metadata(&normalized);
+    let (metadata, patch) = split_patch(remainder);
+
+    Preview {
+        message: Cow::Owned(message.trim_end().to_owned()),
+        metadata: metadata.lines().map(Cow::Borrowed).collect(),
+        patch: patch.map(|value| Cow::Owned(value.to_owned())),
+    }
 }
 
-fn sanitize(line: &str) -> Cow<'_, str> {
-    if line.is_ascii() {
-        Cow::Borrowed(line)
-    } else {
-        Cow::Owned(line.chars().filter(char::is_ascii).collect())
-    }
+fn split_metadata(source: &str) -> (&str, &str) {
+    source
+        .find("\n#")
+        .map(|offset| source.split_at(offset + 1))
+        .unwrap_or((source, ""))
 }
diff --git a/src/commit/parser.rs b/src/commit/parser.rs
index 31a87bc..54ef62a 100644
--- a/src/commit/parser.rs
+++ b/src/commit/parser.rs
@@ -22,12 +22,25 @@ pub fn split_patch(source: &str) -> (&str, Option<&str>) {
-    match source.find("diff --git") {
-        Some(offset) => (&source[..offset], Some(&source[offset..])),
-        None => (source, None),
+    let marker = "diff --git ";
+    for (offset, line) in source.match_indices(marker) {
+        let starts_line = offset == 0 || source.as_bytes()[offset - 1] == b'\n';
+        if starts_line && line == marker {
+            return (&source[..offset], Some(&source[offset..]));
+        }
     }
+    (source, None)
 }
 
+pub fn visible_metadata(source: &str) -> impl Iterator<Item = &str> {
+    source.lines().filter(|line| {
+        line.starts_with("#\tmodified:")
+            || line.starts_with("#\tnew file:")
+            || line.starts_with("#\tdeleted:")
+            || line.starts_with("#\trenamed:")
+    })
+}
+
 #[cfg(test)]
 mod tests {
     use super::*;
diff --git a/tests/commit_preview.rs b/tests/commit_preview.rs
index 77a90af..cf62110 100644
--- a/tests/commit_preview.rs
+++ b/tests/commit_preview.rs
@@ -4,9 +4,22 @@ use mark::commit::preview::render;
 #[test]
-fn strips_non_ascii_from_subject() {
-    let preview = render("release cafe\n".as_bytes());
-    assert_eq!(preview, "release cafe");
+fn preserves_unicode_subject_and_body() {
+    let draft = "Render café, 東京, and rocket 🚀\n\nBody by Zoë.\n";
+    let preview = render(draft.as_bytes());
+    assert_eq!(preview.message, "Render café, 東京, and rocket 🚀\n\nBody by Zoë.");
+    assert!(preview.patch.is_none());
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn separates_git_metadata_from_message() {
+    let draft = concat!(
+        "Keep the subject\n\n",
+        "Explain the reason.\n",
+        "# On branch main\n",
+        "#\tmodified:   src/lib.rs\n",
+    );
+    let preview = render(draft.as_bytes());
+    assert_eq!(preview.message, "Keep the subject\n\nExplain the reason.");
+    assert_eq!(preview.metadata.len(), 2);
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn keeps_verbose_patch_verbatim() {
+    assert!(render(b"Subject\n").patch.is_none());
 }
diff --git a/tests/fixtures/unicode-message.txt b/tests/fixtures/unicode-message.txt
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..b4f81a2
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/fixtures/unicode-message.txt
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+Preserve contributor names and symbols 🚀
+
+The renderer keeps Ελληνικά, 日本語, café, and an astral symbol 𝌆.
+
+# On branch feature/unicode
+# Changes to be committed:
+#\tnew file:   tests/fixtures/unicode-message.txt
